chapter_title.ar: باب الْمَرْأَةِ تُصَلِّي بِحِيَالِ الرَّجُلِ وَ الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي وَ الْمَرْأَةُ بِحِيَالِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 15 – The woman prays Salāt parallel with the man, and the man prays Salāt and the woman is parallel to him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/74)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/74)
text: الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي وَ الْمَرْأَةُ تُصَلِّي بِحِذَاهُ أَوْ إِلَى جَانِبِهِ فَقَالَ إِذَا كَانَ سُجُودُهَا مَعَ رُكُوعِهِ فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from the one who reported it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the man who is praying Salāt and the woman is praying Salāt parallel to him or to his side. So heasws said: ‘If she was performing Sajdah along with his Rukū, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from those who he has mentioned who has said the following: “I once asked abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a woman who performs Salah (prayer) on the side or parallel with a man. He (the Imam) said, ‘If her Sujud (prostration) is with his Ruku‘ (bowing down on one’s knees), it is not harmful.’” (Ahadith 1-7 of this [chapter=Chapter are best explained by fatwa.)
